scenario.name: IEEE_Mag_Step
scenario.description: Scenario A — IEEE Voltage Magnitude Step (+10% at t=0.5s)

params.standard: IEEE Std 60255-118-1:2018 §5.3.4 amplitude-step test; IEEE C37.118.1-2011 §5.4.2 (PMU compliance, magnitude transient).
params.physical_motivation: Represents abrupt voltage sag/swell from fast IBR power injection or capacitor switching in a distribution-tied PV/wind grid. Frequency is held constant to isolate amplitude-frequency cross-coupling errors.
params.ibr_relevance: Grid-following inverters (GFL-IBR) inject current at near-unity PF with sub-millisecond amplitude transients on connect/disconnect. Compliant with IEEE 1547-2018 Category I/II/III amplitude limits.
params.parameters: +10% amplitude step at t=0.5 s; f = 60.0 Hz (constant).
params.noise: Gaussian white noise sigma=0.001 pu (SNR ≈ 57 dB at 1 pu amplitude).
params.dynamics: Piecewise constant amplitude: 1.0 pu → 1.1 pu at t=0.5 s. Phase C0-continuous.
params.exceeds_standard: This is a compliance-level baseline test (not a beyond-standard stress). Serves as a reference point for amplitude-immunity benchmarking.
